name: Sonny Cohn
abstract: George Thomas Cohn (March 14, 1925 – November 7, 2006), known professionally as Sonny Cohn,  was an American jazz trumpeter whose career spanned over six decades. After working for fifteen years with Red Saunders (1945–1960), Cohn went on to spend another twenty-four years in Count Basie's trumpet section (1960–1984).

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie
Kool Jazz Festival: Ella Fitzgerald and Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: I got Eddie Jones in the band, Bill Hughes, Sonny Cohn, Eric Dixon, Thad Jones... Yeah I got a whole lot of people in the band, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I asked our road manager, band manager at that time it was Sonny Cohn... after I’d been with the band about maybe two weeks
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Cohn worked with me for nine years. We worked down in the Downbeat Room.

about_quotes.speaker: Byron Stripling
about_quotes.evidence: I’d see these guys and I’d ask them questions and this and that, and so they sort of knew me from that... I’d write this bass trombone player, Bill Hughes, the manager, or trumpet player at the time, Sonny Cohn
about_quotes.doc_title: Byron Stripling interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Thad Jones, Snooky Young, Sonny Cohn and Lenny Johnson were in the trumpet section and they were making up riffs behind me that had me blowing my buns off.

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: There was only – really, the only three I can think about was King Fleming’s big band, which I was in for a while. That’s where Sonny Cohn come out of.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
